evals.prompt: Bring in the Claude Code session where we researched desktop knowledge management apps last weekend. I want the useful context in this conversation, not a new session.
evals.expected_output: Searches past sessions from the natural-language hint, identifies likely candidates, exports or stages the best matching transcript slice, and imports only the relevant context into the current conversation.

evals.prompt: Resume my most recent Claude session in this repo and fork it so I can continue in a fresh conversation.
evals.expected_output: Uses current-directory context, selects the latest matching session, and resumes it with --fork-session rather than importing transcript text into the active conversation.

evals.prompt: Find the Claude session about oauth in payments-api from earlier this week. There may be more than one, so show me the best options first.
evals.expected_output: Runs a ranked search, presents a short disambiguation list with timestamps/project hints and reasons, and avoids guessing when multiple candidates are close.

evals.prompt: Take that long Claude session from pdfwiki research and save it to a file so we can read it in chunks here.
evals.expected_output: Chooses staged import, exports the transcript to a file, and uses that file as the transport instead of dumping a huge raw transcript directly into the conversation.
